You can set the assigned actions of modifier keys as well as mouse input such as the mouse wheel and right-click.
How to set modifier keys:
1. For each key entry or mouse input, select the action you want to assign from the list.
2. To change the general settings, check [General settings].
3. Click OK to apply your changes.
3. When selecting a sub tool, you can change the input process and output process of that tool. Settings will be shared with other tools that have the same input and output processes. 
4. Click OK to apply your changes.

You can set the assigned actions of modifier keys.

 How to set modifier keys:
 1. For each key entry, select the action you want to assign from the list. 
2. If [General settings] is selected in the list, general settings will be used. To change the general settings, check [General settings].
3. When selecting a sub tool, you can change the input and output processing to be set. Settings will be shared with other tools that have the same input and output processes. 
4. Click OK to apply your changes.

Every time you press key, it will switch between tool and sub tool using the same shortcut.

How to set a shortcut:
1. Select an item and tap [Edit shortcut], then assign the shortcut by pressing the key.
2. Shortcuts can be assigned to tools, sub tool groups, or sub tools.
3. Click [OK] to update the changes.

Set the brush size (line width). 
To add variety to your line width, click the button to the right of the slider to open the [Dynamics] dialog box, where you can link brush size to pen pressure or other dynamics.

The Stabilization and Post-correction settings reduce jitters to create smoother lines. 
 Stabilization smooths lines by delaying the input from the tablet, while Post-correction smooths the line after it is drawn. 
 [Taper] gradually reduces the pen pressure as the pen is lifted from the tablet. When pen pressure is assigned to line width, the line tapers off at the end.

Set the size of the brush.
You can also link the brush size to pen dynamics such as pen pressure by selecting the [Dynamics] button to the right.

Set the thickness of the brush tip.
You can also link the thickness to pen dynamics such as pen pressure by selecting the [Dynamics] button to the right.

When this setting is on, you can snap to rulers, special rulers, and the canvas grid when drawing, according to the settings in the View menu > Snap. This does not apply to sub tools that have this setting turned off.

This setting changes how the [Velocity] is calculated when used as input for tool dynamics. When turned on, the change caused by the speed of the pen will be consistent across different devices. When turned off, velocity will be calculated as in previous versions.
Change [Brush shape] for current sub tool.

Save the current brush shape as a preset. 
Once saved as a preset, you can use it for other sub tools.
Moves the start points of the grid and ruler bar (shown using the [View] menu). This tool does not move layers.

When this setting is on, you can align and snap to objects when dragging objects or handles,  according to the settings in the View menu > Snap. When this setting is turned off, the sub tool will not snap to other objects.

When this option is turned on, the lines will be created with extra control points for adjusting the curve. After making the lines, click with the [Object] sub tool to adjust the curve.
If you want straight lines, leave this box unchecked.

Select whether changes to the Tool Property palette apply to new text or selected text. When set to “New only”, changes in the Tool Property will not affect any selected text. When set to “Selected text”, the properties will apply to new and selected text.
When this setting is turned off, the sub tool will not snap to symmetrical rulers even when snapping is enabled.

Set the fog type. 
Depth fog becomes thicker evenly according to the distance from the screen. 
Distance fog becomes thicker spherically according to the distance from the camera.
